records.chapter: Chapter on Refusing to Accept a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5446 - Ribʿi narrated from Al-Fuḍayl ibn Yasar from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who is appointed as an executor.
Imam (as) said: "If the will is sent to him from another town, he does not have the right to reject it. However, if he is in a city where others are available, then it is up to him to accept or decline it."
records.arabicText: 5446 - وَ رَوَى رِبْعِيٌّ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ يُوصَى إِلَيْهِ قَالَ "إِذَا بُعِثَ بِهَا إِلَيْهِ مِنْ بَلَدٍ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ رَدُّهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي مِصْرٍ يُوجَدُ فِيهِ غَيْرُهُ فَذَاكَ إِلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5446 - Ribʿi narrated from Al-Fuḍayl ibn Yasar from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who is appointed as an executor.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the will is sent to him from another town, he does not have the right to reject it. However, if he is in a city where others are available, then it is up to him to accept or decline it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Refusing to Accept a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5447 - Sahl ibn Ziyad narrated from Ali ibn Al-Rayyan, who said:
I wrote to Abu Al-Hasan (as) asking about a man whose father invited him to accept his will. Is it permissible for him to refuse accepting his father's will?
Imam (as) replied: "He does not have the right to refuse."
records.arabicText: 5447 - وَ رَوَى سَهْلُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلرَّيَّانِ قَالَ : كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ دَعَاهُ وَالِدُهُ إِلَى قَبُولِ وَصِيَّتِهِ هَلْ لَهُ أَنْ يَمْتَنِعَ مِنْ قَبُولِ وَصِيَّةِ وَالِدِهِ فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَمْتَنِعَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5447 - Sahl ibn Ziyad narrated from Ali ibn Al-Rayyan, who said:
I wrote to Abu Al-Hasan (as) asking about a man whose father invited him to accept his will. Is it permissible for him to refuse accepting his father's will?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He does not have the right to refuse."

records.chapter: Chapter on Refusing to Accept a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5448 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who is entrusted with a will by another man but dislikes accepting it.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "He should not abandon him in this situation."
records.arabicText: 5448 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُوصِي إِلَى اَلرَّجُلِ بِوَصِيَّةٍ فَيَكْرَهُ أَنْ يَقْبَلَهَا فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لاَ يَخْذُلْهُ عَلَى هَذِهِ اَلْحَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5448 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who is entrusted with a will by another man but dislikes accepting it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (as) said: "He should not abandon him in this situation."

records.chapter: Chapter on Refusing to Accept a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5449 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Sayf ibn Umayrah from Manṣur ibn Hazim from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"If a man appoints his brother as his executor while he is absent, he does not have the right to reject the will. This is because if he had been present and refused to accept it, the testator would have sought someone else."
records.arabicText: 5449 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا أَوْصَى اَلرَّجُلُ إِلَى أَخِيهِ وَ هُوَ غَائِبٌ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَرُدَّ وَصِيَّتَهُ لِأَنَّهُ لَوْ كَانَ شَاهِداً فَأَبَى أَنْ يَقْبَلَهَا طَلَبَ غَيْرَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5449 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Sayf ibn Umayrah from Manṣur ibn Hazim from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man appoints his brother as his executor while he is absent, he does not have the right to reject the will. This is because if he had been present and refused to accept it, the testator would have sought someone else."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Age at Which a child's Bequest Becomes Valid
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5450 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Aban ibn Uthman from Abd Al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"When a boy reaches the age of ten, his will becomes valid."
records.arabicText: 5450 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِذَا بَلَغَ اَلْغُلاَمُ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5450 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Aban ibn Uthman from Abd Al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a boy reaches the age of ten, his will becomes valid."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Age at Which a child's Bequest Becomes Valid
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5451 - Ṣafwan ibn Yahya narrated from Musa ibn Bakr from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"When a boy reaches the age of ten, whatever he does with his wealth - whether he frees a slave, gives charity, or makes a will within reasonable limits and rightful matters - is valid."
records.arabicText: 5451 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَ قَالَ : "إِذَا أَتَى عَلَى اَلْغُلاَمِ عَشْرُ سِنِينَ فَإِنَّهُ يَجُوزُ لَهُ فِي مَالِهِ مَا أَعْتَقَ أَوْ تَصَدَّقَ وَ أَوْصَى عَلَى حَدٍّ مَعْرُوفٍ وَ حَقٍّ فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5451 - Ṣafwan ibn Yahya narrated from Musa ibn Bakr from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a boy reaches the age of ten, whatever he does with his wealth - whether he frees a slave, gives charity, or makes a will within reasonable limits and rightful matters - is valid."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Age at Which a child's Bequest Becomes Valid
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5452 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abi Al-Maghra from Abu Baṣir from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said: "If a boy reaches the age of ten and makes a will for one-third of his wealth in a rightful cause, his will is valid. And if he is seven years old and makes a will for a small amount of his wealth in a rightful cause, his will is also valid."
records.arabicText: 5452 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْمَغْرَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِذَا بَلَغَ اَلْغُلاَمُ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ فَأَوْصَى بِثُلُثِ مَالِهِ فِي حَقٍّ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ وَ إِذَا كَانَ اِبْنَ سَبْعِ سِنِينَ فَأَوْصَى مِنْ مَالِهِ بِالْيَسِيرِ فِي حَقٍّ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5452 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abi Al-Maghra from Abu Baṣir from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a boy reaches the age of ten and makes a will for one-third of his wealth in a rightful cause, his will is valid. And if he is seven years old and makes a will for a small amount of his wealth in a rightful cause, his will is also valid."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Age at Which a child's Bequest Becomes Valid
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5453 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Dawud ibn Al-Nuʿman from Abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
"If a boy is on his deathbed and makes a will before reaching maturity, his will is valid if it is for his close relatives, but it is not valid for strangers."
records.arabicText: 5453 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ اَلنُّعْمَانِ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "إِنَّ اَلْغُلاَمَ إِذَا حَضَرَهُ اَلْمَوْتُ فَأَوْصَى وَ لَمْ يُدْرِكْ جَازَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ لِذَوِي اَلْأَرْحَامِ وَ لَمْ تَجُزْ لِلْغُرَبَاءِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5453 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Dawud ibn Al-Nuʿman from Abu Ayyub from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a boy is on his deathbed and makes a will before reaching maturity, his will is valid if it is for his close relatives, but it is not valid for strangers."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests Made Through Writing and Gestures
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5454 - Abd Al-Ṣamad ibn Muhammad narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir from his father, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"I entered upon Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Al-Hanafiyya, and his tongue had become paralyzed.
I instructed him to make his will, but he did not respond. So, I ordered a basin to be brought and filled it with sand.
I said to him, 'Write with your hand,' and he wrote his will in the sand with his hand, and I copied it onto a scroll."
records.arabicText:
5454 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلصَّمَدِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ حَنَانِ بْنِ سَدِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
"دَخَلْتُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ اِبْنِ اَلْحَنَفِيَّةِ وَ قَدِ اُعْتُقِلَ لِسَانُهُ فَأَمَرْتُهُ بِالْوَصِيَّةِ فَلَمْ يُجِبْ"
قَالَ "فَأَمَرْتُ بِطَسْتٍ فَجَعَلْتُ فِيهِ اَلرَّمْلَ فَوُضِعَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ خُطَّ بِيَدِكَ فَخَطَّ وَصِيَّتَهُ بِيَدِهِ فِي اَلرَّمْلِ وَ نَسَخْتُ أَنَا فِي صَحِيفَةٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5454 - Abd Al-Ṣamad ibn Muhammad narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir from his father, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I entered upon Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Al-Hanafiyya, and his tongue had become paralyzed.
I instructed him to make his will, but he did not respond. So, I ordered a basin to be brought and filled it with sand.
I said to him, 'Write with your hand,' and he wrote his will in the sand with his hand, and I copied it onto a scroll."

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests Made Through Writing and Gestures
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5455 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad Al-Ashari narrated from Al-Sindi ibn Muhammad from Yunus ibn Yaʿqub from Abu Maryam, who narrated from his father:
Umamah bint Abi Al-Aṣ - whose mother was Zaynab, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) - was married to Imam Ali ibn Abi Ṭalib (as) after Sayyida Faṭimah {s.a} (passed away).
After the martyrdom of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), Al-Mughirah ibn Al-Nawfal married her.
It was narrated that she became severely ill until her tongue became paralyzed. Imam Hasan ibn Ali (as) and Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as), the sons of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), came to her while she was unable to speak.
They (as) began asking her, while Al-Mughirah disapproved.
They (as) asked: "Did you emancipate so-and-so and his family?" She nodded her head in agreement.
They (as) continued: "And this and that?"
She again nodded her head in affirmation but could not speak clearly.
Her gestures were accepted as valid in confirming her wishes.
records.arabicText:
5455 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ اَلْأَشْعَرِيُّ عَنِ اَلسِّنْدِيِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ عَنْ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ ذَكَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِيهِ :
أَنَّ أُمَامَةَ بِنْتَ أَبِي اَلْعَاصِ وَ أُمُّهَا زَيْنَبُ بِنْتُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَانَتْ تَحْتَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بَعْدَ فَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ فَخَلَفَ عَلَيْهَا بَعْدَ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ اَلنَّوْفَلِ فَذَكَرَ أَنَّهَا وَجِعَتْ وَجَعاً شَدِيداً حَتَّى اُعْتُقِلَ لِسَانُهَا فَجَاءَهَا اَلْحَسَنُ وَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ اِبْنَا عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ هِيَ لاَ تَسْتَطِيعُ اَلْكَلاَمَ فَجَعَلاَ يَقُولاَنِ لَهَا وَ اَلْمُغِيرَةُ كَارِهٌ لِذَلِكَ "أَعْتَقْتِ فُلاَناً وَ أَهْلَهُ"
فَجَعَلَتْ تُشِيرُ بِرَأْسِهَا نَعَمْ "وَ كَذَا وَ كَذَا" فَجَعَلَتْ تُشِيرُ بِرَأْسِهَا أَنْ نَعَمْ لاَ تُفْصِحُ بِالْكَلاَمِ فَأَجَازَ ذَلِكَ لَهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5455 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad Al-Ashari narrated from Al-Sindi ibn Muhammad from Yunus ibn Yaʿqub from Abu Maryam, who narrated from his father:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Umamah bint Abi Al-Aṣ - whose mother was Zaynab, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) - was married to Imam Ali ibn Abi Ṭalib (as) after Sayyida Faṭimah {s.a} (passed away).
After the martyrdom of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), Al-Mughirah ibn Al-Nawfal married her.
It was narrated that she became severely ill until her tongue became paralyzed. Imam Hasan ibn Ali (as) and Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as), the sons of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), came to her while she was unable to speak.
They (as) began asking her, while Al-Mughirah disapproved.
They (as) asked: "Did you emancipate so-and-so and his family?" She nodded her head in agreement.
They (as) continued: "And this and that?"
She again nodded her head in affirmation but could not speak clearly.
Her gestures were accepted as valid in confirming her wishes.

records.chapter: Chapter on Bequests Made Through Writing and Gestures
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5456 - It was narrated from Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Al-Hamadhani, who said:
I wrote to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) regarding a man who wrote something in his own handwriting but did not tell his heirs, "This is my will," nor did he say, "I have made a will." However, he wrote a document that contained what he intended to bequeath.
I asked whether it was obligatory for his heirs to fulfill what was written in his handwriting, even though he did not command them to do so.
Abu Al-Hasan (as) replied: "If he had children, they must carry out everything they find written in their father's document, whether it pertains to acts of righteousness or anything else."
records.arabicText: 5456 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْهَمَذَانِيِّ قَالَ : كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ كَتَبَ كِتَاباً بِخَطِّهِ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لِوَرَثَتِهِ هَذِهِ وَصِيَّتِي وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ إِنِّي قَدْ أَوْصَيْتُ إِلاَّ أَنَّهُ كَتَبَ كِتَاباً فِيهِ مَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يُوصِيَ بِهِ هَلْ يَجِبُ عَلَى وَرَثَتِهِ اَلْقِيَامُ بِمَا فِي اَلْكِتَابِ بِخَطِّهِ وَ لَمْ يَأْمُرْهُمْ بِذَلِكَ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ وُلْدٌ يُنْفِذُونَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ يَجِدُونَ فِي كِتَابِ أَبِيهِمْ فِي وَجْهِ اَلْبِرِّ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5456 - It was narrated from Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Al-Hamadhani, who said:
I wrote to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) regarding a man who wrote something in his own handwriting but did not tell his heirs, "This is my will," nor did he say, "I have made a will." However, he wrote a document that contained what he intended to bequeath.
I asked whether it was obligatory for his heirs to fulfill what was written in his handwriting, even though he did not command them to do so.
Abu Al-Hasan (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he had children, they must carry out everything they find written in their father's document, whether it pertains to acts of righteousness or anything else."

records.chapter: Chapter on Revoking a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5457 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faḍḍal narrated from Ali ibn Uqbah from Burayd Al-ʿIjli from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"The one who makes a will has the right to revoke it or make changes to it as long as he is alive."
records.arabicText: 5457 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ اَلْعِجْلِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لِصَاحِبِ اَلْوَصِيَّةِ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِيهَا وَ يُحْدِثَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ مَا دَامَ حَيّاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5457 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faḍḍal narrated from Ali ibn Uqbah from Burayd Al-ʿIjli from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The one who makes a will has the right to revoke it or make changes to it as long as he is alive."

records.chapter: Chapter on Revoking a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5458 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Bukayr ibn Aʿyan from Ubayd ibn Zurarah, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
"The one making a will has the right to revoke it, whether he is in good health or ill."
records.arabicText: 5458 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "لِلْمُوصِي أَنْ يَرْجِعَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ إِنْ كَانَ فِي صِحَّةٍ أَوْ مَرَضٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5458 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Bukayr ibn Aʿyan from Ubayd ibn Zurarah, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The one making a will has the right to revoke it, whether he is in good health or ill."

records.chapter: Chapter on Revoking a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5459 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled that the manumitted slave (Al-mudabbar) is to be freed from one-third of the estate. A man has the right to revoke his will, to add to it or reduce from it, as long as he has not passed away."
records.arabicText: 5459 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ اَلْمُدَبَّرَ مِنَ اَلثُّلُثِ وَ أَنَّ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يَنْقُضَ وَصِيَّتَهُ فَيَزِيدَ فِيهَا وَ يَنْقُصَ مِنْهَا مَا لَمْ يَمُتْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5459 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled that the manumitted slave (Al-mudabbar) is to be freed from one-third of the estate. A man has the right to revoke his will, to add to it or reduce from it, as long as he has not passed away."

records.chapter: Chapter on Revoking a Bequest
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5460 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman with his chain of transmission, Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
"A man has the right to alter his will. He may free the one he had initially ordered to be owned, and he may grant ownership to the one he had initially ordered to be freed. He may give to the one he had previously deprived and deprive the one he had previously granted, as long as he has not retracted from his decision."
records.arabicText: 5460 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُغَيِّرَ مِنْ وَصِيَّتِهِ فَيُعْتِقَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِتَمْلِيكِهِ وَ يُمَلِّكَ مَنْ كَانَ أَمَرَ بِعِتْقِهِ وَ يُعْطِيَ مَنْ كَانَ حَرَمَهُ وَ يَحْرِمَ مَنْ كَانَ أَعْطَاهُ مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ رَجَعَ عَنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5460 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman with his chain of transmission, Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man has the right to alter his will. He may free the one he had initially ordered to be owned, and he may grant ownership to the one he had initially ordered to be freed. He may give to the one he had previously deprived and deprive the one he had previously granted, as long as he has not retracted from his decision."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Makes a Bequest Exceeding One-Third While His Heirs Are Present and Approve it, if They Can Revoke it After His Death
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5461 - Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who made a will in the presence of his heirs, and they approved it. However, after the man's death, they attempted to revoke the will.
Imam (as) said: "They do not have the right to revoke it. The will remains binding upon them once they had approved it during his lifetime."
records.arabicText: 5461 - رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ وَ وَرَثَتُهُ شُهُودٌ فَأَجَازُوا ذَلِكَ فَلَمَّا مَاتَ اَلرَّجُلُ نَقَضُوا اَلْوَصِيَّةَ هَلْ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَرُدُّوا مَا أَقَرُّوا بِهِ فَقَالَ "لَيْسَ لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ وَ اَلْوَصِيَّةُ جَائِزَةٌ عَلَيْهِمْ إِذَا أَقَرُّوا بِهَا فِي حَيَاتِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5461 - Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who made a will in the presence of his heirs, and they approved it. However, after the man's death, they attempted to revoke the will.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "They do not have the right to revoke it. The will remains binding upon them once they had approved it during his lifetime."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Obligation of Executing the Bequest and the Prohibition of Altering it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5462 - Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who made a will to give his wealth in the way of Allah (swt).
Imam (as) replied: "Give it to the one he specified in his will, even if that person is a Jew or a Christian. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, says:
'But whoever changes it after hearing it, the sin is only upon those who change it.'"
(Surah Al-Baqarah 2:181)
records.arabicText:
5462 - رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ :
سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ أَوْصَى بِمَالِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ "أَعْطِهِ لِمَنْ أَوْصَى لَهُ بِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ يَهُودِيّاً أَوْ نَصْرَانِيّاً إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ
۝ فَمَنْ بَدَّلَهُ بَعْدَ مٰا سَمِعَهُ فَإِنَّمٰا إِثْمُهُ عَلَى اَلَّذِينَ يُبَدِّلُونَهُ ۝.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5462 - Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who made a will to give his wealth in the way of Allah (swt).
Imam (as) replied: "Give it to the one he specified in his will, even if that person is a Jew or a Christian. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, says:
'But whoever changes it after hearing it, the sin is only upon those who change it.'"
(Surah Al-Baqarah 2:181)

records.chapter: Chapter on the Obligation of Executing the Bequest and the Prohibition of Altering it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5463 - Sahl ibn Ziyad narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Walid from Yunus ibn Yaʿqub:
A man in Hamadhan mentioned that his father had passed away. His father was unaware of this matter (the teachings of the Ahl Al-Bayt), and before his death, he made a will instructing that something be given in the way of Allah (swt).
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about how to fulfill this will, and it was explained to Imam (as) that the deceased was unaware of the truth but had made a will at the time of his death.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "If a man had made a will to me to distribute his wealth to a Jew or a Christian, I would fulfill it for them. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, says:
'But whoever changes it after hearing it, the sin is only upon those who change it. Verily, Allah (swt) is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.'" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:181)
Then Imam (as) added: "Look for someone who is engaged in these paths (meaning the frontier defense posts), and send it to him."
records.arabicText:
5463 - وَ رَوَى سَهْلُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْوَلِيدِ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ :
أَنَّ رَجُلاً كَانَ بِهَمَذَانَ ذَكَرَ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ مَاتَ وَ كَانَ لاَ يَعْرِفُ هَذَا اَلْأَمْرَ فَأَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ عِنْدَ اَلْمَوْتِ وَ أَوْصَى أَنْ يُعْطَى شَيْءٌ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ فَسُئِلَ عَنْهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَيْفَ يُفْعَلُ بِهِ وَ أَخْبَرْنَاهُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لاَ يَعْرِفُ هَذَا اَلْأَمْرَ وَ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ عِنْدَ اَلْمَوْتِ فَقَالَ "لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً أَوْصَى إِلَيَّ أَنْ أَضَعَ مَالَهُ فِي يَهُودِيٍّ أَوْ نَصْرَانِيٍّ لَوَضَعْتُهُ فِيهِمْ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ
۝ فَمَنْ بَدَّلَهُ بَعْدَ مٰا سَمِعَهُ فَإِنَّمٰا إِثْمُهُ عَلَى اَلَّذِينَ يُبَدِّلُونَهُ إِنَّ اَللّٰهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ ۝
فَانْظُرْ إِلَى مَنْ يَخْرُجُ فِي هَذِهِ اَلْوُجُوهِ يَعْنِي اَلثُّغُورَ فَابْعَثُوا بِهِ إِلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5463 - Sahl ibn Ziyad narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Walid from Yunus ibn Yaʿqub:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man in Hamadhan mentioned that his father had passed away. His father was unaware of this matter (the teachings of the Ahl Al-Bayt), and before his death, he made a will instructing that something be given in the way of Allah (swt).
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about how to fulfill this will, and it was explained to Imam (as) that the deceased was unaware of the truth but had made a will at the time of his death.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "If a man had made a will to me to distribute his wealth to a Jew or a Christian, I would fulfill it for them. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, says:
'But whoever changes it after hearing it, the sin is only upon those who change it. Verily, Allah (swt) is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.'" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:181)
Then Imam (as) added: "Look for someone who is engaged in these paths (meaning the frontier defense posts), and send it to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Obligation of Executing the Bequest and the Prohibition of Altering it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5464 - It was narrated from Abu Ṭalib Abdullah ibn Al-Ṣalt Al-Qummi that he said:
Al-Khalil ibn Hashim wrote to Dhu Al-Ri'asatayn, who was the governor of Nishapur, informing him that a man from the Magians had passed away and had bequeathed a portion of his wealth to the poor.
The executor in Nishapur took that wealth and distributed it among the poor Muslims.
Al-Khalil wrote to Dhu Al-Riʾasatayn about this matter, and Dhu Al-Riʾasatayn consulted Al-Maʾmun, who said: "I have no ruling on this issue."
Then he asked Abu Al-Hasan (as), and Abu Al-Hasan (as) replied:
"The Magian did not bequeath his wealth to the poor Muslims. Therefore, it is appropriate that an equivalent amount from the charity funds be taken and returned to the poor among the Magians."
records.arabicText: 5464 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي طَالِبٍ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ اَلصَّلْتِ اَلْقُمِّيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : كَتَبَ اَلْخَلِيلُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ إِلَى ذِي اَلرِّئَاسَتَيْنِ وَ هُوَ وَالِي نَيْسَابُورَ أَنَّ رَجُلاً مِنَ اَلْمَجُوسِ مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى لِلْفُقَرَاءِ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ مَالِهِ فَأَخَذَهُ اَلْوَصِيُّ بِنَيْسَابُورَ فَجَعَلَهُ فِي فُقَرَاءِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَكَتَبَ اَلْخَلِيلُ إِلَى ذِي اَلرِّئَاسَتَيْنِ بِذَلِكَ فَسَأَلَ اَلْمَأْمُونَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ لَيْسَ عِنْدِي فِي ذَلِكَ شَيْءٌ فَسَأَلَ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "إِنَّ اَلْمَجُوسِيَّ لَمْ يُوصِ لِفُقَرَاءِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ لَكِنْ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يُؤْخَذَ مِقْدَارُ ذَلِكَ اَلْمَالِ مِنْ مَالِ اَلصَّدَقَةِ فَيُرَدَّ عَلَى فُقَرَاءِ اَلْمَجُوسِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5464 - It was narrated from Abu Ṭalib Abdullah ibn Al-Ṣalt Al-Qummi that he said:
Al-Khalil ibn Hashim wrote to Dhu Al-Ri'asatayn, who was the governor of Nishapur, informing him that a man from the Magians had passed away and had bequeathed a portion of his wealth to the poor.
The executor in Nishapur took that wealth and distributed it among the poor Muslims.
Al-Khalil wrote to Dhu Al-Riʾasatayn about this matter, and Dhu Al-Riʾasatayn consulted Al-Maʾmun, who said: "I have no ruling on this issue."
Then he asked Abu Al-Hasan (as), and Abu Al-Hasan (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Magian did not bequeath his wealth to the poor Muslims. Therefore, it is appropriate that an equivalent amount from the charity funds be taken and returned to the poor among the Magians."

records.chapter: Chapter on a person's Right Over His Wealth as Long as There Remains Some Life in Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5466 - Abdullah ibn Jabalah narrated from Samaah, from Abu Baṣir, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as), "A man who has children, is it permissible for him to give his wealth to his relatives?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is his wealth; he may do with it as he wishes until death comes to him."
records.arabicText:
5466 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ جَبَلَةَ عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
قُلْتُ لَهُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ لَهُ اَلْوَلَدُ يَسَعُهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ مَالَهُ لِقَرَابَتِهِ قَالَ "هُوَ مَالُهُ يَصْنَعُ بِهِ مَا شَاءَ إِلَى أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُ اَلْمَوْتُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5466 - Abdullah ibn Jabalah narrated from Samaah, from Abu Baṣir, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as), "A man who has children, is it permissible for him to give his wealth to his relatives?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is his wealth; he may do with it as he wishes until death comes to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a person's Right Over His Wealth as Long as There Remains Some Life in Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5467 - And the confirmation of this is what Ṣafwan narrated from Murazim regarding a man who gives something from his wealth during his illness.
Imm (as) said: "If he clearly transfers it (during his lifetime), then it is valid. However, if he bequeaths it (for after his death), then it is only from one-third of his wealth."
records.arabicText: 5467 - وَ تَصْدِيقُ ذَلِكَ- مَا رَوَاهُ صَفْوَانُ عَنْ مُرَازِمٍ‌ فِي الرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي الشَّيْ‌ءَ مِنْ مَالِهِ فِي مَرَضِهِ قَالَ إِذَا أَبَانَ بِهِ فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ وَ إِنْ أَوْصَى بِهِ فَمِنَ الثُّلُثِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5467 - And the confirmation of this is what Ṣafwan narrated from Murazim regarding a man who gives something from his wealth during his illness.
Imm (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he clearly transfers it (during his lifetime), then it is valid. However, if he bequeaths it (for after his death), then it is only from one-third of his wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on a person's Right Over His Wealth as Long as There Remains Some Life in Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5468 - As for the narration of Ali ibn Asbaṭ from Thaʿlabah from Abu Al-Hasan Amr ibn Shaddad Al-Azdi from Ammar ibn Musa from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A man has the most right over his wealth as long as his soul remains in him. If he bequeaths all of it, it is permissible for him."
records.arabicText:
5468 - وَ أَمَّا حَدِيثُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ ثَعْلَبَةَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ شَدَّادٍ اَلْأَزْدِيِّ عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ:
"اَلرَّجُلُ أَحَقُّ بِمَالِهِ مَا دَامَ فِيهِ اَلرُّوحُ إِنْ أَوْصَى بِهِ كُلِّهِ فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5468 - As for the narration of Ali ibn Asbaṭ from Thaʿlabah from Abu Al-Hasan Amr ibn Shaddad Al-Azdi from Ammar ibn Musa from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man has the most right over his wealth as long as his soul remains in him. If he bequeaths all of it, it is permissible for him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a person's Right Over His Wealth as Long as There Remains Some Life in Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5469 - And a confirmation of this is what was narrated by Ismaʿil ibn Abi Ziyad Al-Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Abu Abdullah Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he was asked about a man who dies without leaving behind an heir or any male relative (Aṣabah).
Imam (as) said: "He may bequeath his wealth as he wishes among the Muslims, the poor, and the wayfarer."
records.arabicText: 5469 - وَ تَصْدِيقُ ذَلِكَ مَا رَوَاهُ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَمُوتُ وَ لاَ وَارِثَ لَهُ وَ لاَ عَصَبَةَ قَالَ "يُوصِي بِمَالِهِ حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ فِي اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ اَلْمَسَاكِينِ وَ اِبْنِ اَلسَّبِيلِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Bequest of One Who Intentionally Takes His Own Life
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5470 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
"Whoever intentionally kills himself will be in the Fire of Hell, abiding therein forever."
Imam (as) was asked: "What if he had made a will (waṣiyyah) and then deliberately killed himself immediately after? Will his will be carried out?"
Imam (as) replied: "If he made the will before committing any harmful act against himself, whether by injury or any other action, then his will is valid regarding one-third of his wealth. However, if he made the will after having already harmed himself - by injuring himself or committing an act intending death - then his will is not valid."
records.arabicText: 5470 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ مُتَعَمِّداً فَهُوَ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ "خٰالِداً فِيهٰا" " قِيلَ لَهُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ ثُمَّ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ مُتَعَمِّداً مِنْ سَاعَتِهِ تُنْفَذُ وَصِيَّتُهُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ أَوْصَى قَبْلَ أَنْ يُحْدِثَ حَدَثاً فِي نَفْسِهِ مِنْ جِرَاحَةٍ أَوْ فِعْلٍ أُجِيزَتْ وَصِيَّتُهُ فِي ثُلُثِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ وَ قَدْ أَحْدَثَ فِي نَفْسِهِ جِرَاحَةً أَوْ فِعْلاً لَعَلَّهُ يَمُوتُ لَمْ تُجَزْ وَصِيَّتُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5470 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Walad, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever intentionally kills himself will be in the Fire of Hell, abiding therein forever."
Imam (as) was asked: "What if he had made a will (waṣiyyah) and then deliberately killed himself immediately after? Will his will be carried out?"
Imam (as) replied: "If he made the will before committing any harmful act against himself, whether by injury or any other action, then his will is valid regarding one-third of his wealth. However, if he made the will after having already harmed himself - by injuring himself or committing an act intending death - then his will is not valid."

records.chapter: Chapter on Two Men Being Appointed as Executors of a Bequest, and Each Taking Charge of Half of the Estate
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5471 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Ṣaffar (may Allah (swt) be pleased with him) wrote to Abu Muhammad Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as) asking:
"A man appointed two individuals as executors of his will. Is it permissible for one of them to independently handle half of the inheritance and the other to manage the other half?"
Imam (as) responded in writing: "It is not appropriate for them to act contrary to the deceased's instructions. They should act according to what he commanded them, God willing."
records.arabicText: 5471 - كَتَبَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْحَسَنِ اَلصَّفَّارُ رَضِيَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : رَجُلٌ أَوْصَى إِلَى رَجُلَيْنِ أَ يَجُوزُ لِأَحَدِهِمَا أَنْ يَنْفَرِدَ بِنِصْفِ اَلتَّرِكَةِ وَ اَلْآخَرِ بِالنِّصْفِ فَوَقَّعَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لاَ يَنْبَغِي لَهُمَا أَنْ يُخَالِفَا اَلْمَيِّتَ وَ يَعْمَلاَنِ عَلَى حَسَبِ مَا أَمَرَهُمَا إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5471 - Muhammad ibn Al-Hasan Al-Ṣaffar (may Allah (swt) be pleased with him) wrote to Abu Muhammad Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al-Askari (as) asking:
"A man appointed two individuals as executors of his will. Is it permissible for one of them to independently handle half of the inheritance and the other to manage the other half?"
Imam (as) responded in writing:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not appropriate for them to act contrary to the deceased's instructions. They should act according to what he commanded them, God willing."
